figures.caption: Figure 1: Overview of D-Shap. (a) D-Shap represents Shapley values as a player-by-task matrix Φ, where Φi,j = ϕ(zi, tj) denotes player zi’s contribution to task tj . (b) Task-incremental update (Sec. 3.3): a new task t′ is valued by interpolating nearby columns (green) under the model-induced distance dΓ. (c) Player-incremental update (Sec. 3.4): a new player z′ affects only columns whose local games involve it (coral); other entries (gray) stay frozen.
Table 8: Effect of support set size |N (t)|.
